column1: What is the primary goal of the application developed in the session?
column2: Generating LinkedIn posts using AI based on a topic and tone.

column1: Which AI platform is used in the session to obtain the large language model (LLM) functionality via API?
column2: Groq

column1: Which library is utilized to create the web-based user interface for the AI application?
column2: Gradio

column1: In the hotel analogy for APIs, what component represents the front-end 'dish' presented to the customer?
column2: The user interface where the final result is displayed.

column1: In the hotel analogy for APIs, what represents the back-end 'kitchen' where the data is processed?
column2: The server or AI model logic.

column1: In the hotel analogy, the _____ acts as the API, communicating between the user (customer) and the server (kitchen).
column2: Waiter

column1: What are the three main steps identified for building and launching the GenAI application?
column2: Generating code with GPT, testing in Google Colab, and deploying on Hugging Face.

column1: What is the purpose of using Google Colab in the application development workflow?
column2: Testing and debugging the code generated by GPT in a live environment.

column1: Why is it important to save the Groq API key immediately after creating it?
column2: It is only displayed once and cannot be viewed again later for security reasons.

column1: What should a developer do if copy-pasted code from ChatGPT results in an error during testing?
column2: Copy the error message and ask ChatGPT to provide a solution or updated code.

column1: To keep an API key private in Google Colab, the developer should use the _____ menu.
column2: Secrets (indicated by a key icon)

column1: What Python module is used to retrieve secret keys stored in Google Colab's 'Secrets' section?
column2: google.colab.userdata

column1: Which Hugging Face feature is used to host and run interactive machine learning web applications?
column2: Spaces

column1: When deploying to Hugging Face, what is the standard name for the Python file containing the main application logic?
column2: app.py

column1: What is the purpose of the 'requirements.txt' file in a Hugging Face deployment?
column2: Listing all external libraries (like gradio and groq) that the app needs to install.

column1: Where in the Hugging Face settings can you securely store API keys so they are not visible in the public code?
column2: Variables and secrets

column1: When setting a 'Secret' in Hugging Face, the 'Name' field must exactly match the _____ used in the Python code.
column2: Environment variable name

column1: What was the cause of the 'Invalid API Key' error during the session's live demonstration?
column2: The developer forgot to paste the actual API key value into the code or secret field.

column1: How does providing a specific 'tone' (e.g., professional, motivational) improve AI post generation?
column2: It allows the AI to adjust its language style to meet specific audience expectations.

column1: What is the primary risk of hardcoding an API key directly into a public Google Colab notebook?
column2: Unauthorized users can see the key, use the service at the owner's expense, or compromise security.

column1: Which Gradio component allows users to type in the subject they want a post generated for?
column2: Textbox (or Input field)

column1: To improve the visual look of a Gradio app, what technology can be used for custom styling like colors and layout?
column2: Custom CSS

column1: What common error occurs when an AI model name in the code is outdated or no longer supported?
column2: Model not found (or Authentication/API errors).

column1: Why is Gradio preferred for simple GenAI prototypes over building a full website from scratch?
column2: It provides ready-to-use UI components that require very little code to connect to Python functions.

column1: How can a developer allow users to easily use the generated LinkedIn post text?
column2: By implementing a 'copy to clipboard' function or display field in the UI.

column1: What is the limitation of the Gradio live link generated in Google Colab?
column2: It is temporary and typically expires after 72 hours (or a short period).

column1: Which file type on Hugging Face provides documentation and metadata about the Space?
column2: README.md

column1: True or False: A web application built with Gradio and deployed on Hugging Face can be accessed via a mobile browser.
column2: True

column1: To create a mobile application (native app) instead of a web app, what are two recommended technologies mentioned?
column2: Flutter and React Native.

column1: What specific Python command is used in the session to install necessary libraries in Colab?
column2: !pip install

column1: Which Hugging Face SDK option should be selected when creating a new Space for a Gradio app?
column2: Gradio

column1: What is the function of 'committing' a file in Hugging Face?
column2: Saving the changes and uploading the file to the Space's repository.

column1: Concept: Environment Variables
column2: Definition: Dynamic values (like API keys) stored outside the application code to enhance security and flexibility.

column1: What does the 'Public' setting on a Hugging Face Space allow?
column2: Anyone with the URL can access and use the deployed application.

column1: Why might a LinkedIn post generator require prompt engineering instructions like 'add 4-6 hashtags'?
column2: To ensure the AI output follows platform-specific best practices for reach and engagement.

column1: If an application on Hugging Face shows a 'Runtime Error', where should the developer look for details?
column2: The application logs (usually found by clicking the 'Logs' or error status button).

column1: In the context of Groq, what does the API provide that the local computer usually lacks?
column2: Access to high-performance GPUs and pre-trained Large Language Models.

column1: What is the benefit of generating code 'cell by cell' for Google Colab?
column2: It makes it easier to test individual parts of the code and isolate errors.

column1: In the code, what does the `gr.Interface` function typically define?
column2: The input components, output components, and the main function to be called.

column1: What is the significance of the `requirements.txt` file being separate from `app.py`?
column2: It tells the server environment which specific dependencies to install before running the script.

column1: Which section of the Groq website is used to generate a free API key?
column2: The Developer (or Console) section under 'API Keys'.

column1: How can a developer make their Gradio app look more 'professional' according to the session?
column2: By adding custom headers, using themes, or organizing inputs into a cleaner layout.

column1: Why are LLM API keys often 'paid' services in a commercial setting?
column2: Running large models requires significant computational resources (GPUs) and electricity.

column1: In the Gradio UI, what component is usually used for selecting from a list of tones like 'Casual' or 'Formal'?
column2: Dropdown (or Radio buttons).

column1: What does 'deployment' mean in the context of this software development workflow?
column2: Moving the application from a local or testing environment to a live server where users can access it.

column1: Why does the instructor recommend creating separate API keys for different applications?
column2: To keep usage records organized and track which application is consuming more resources.

column1: In the provided example, what happens when a user clicks the 'Generate' button?
column2: The UI sends the topic and tone to the Python function, which calls the Groq API to get the text.

column1: What is the first thing to check if a Hugging Face Space says 'Groq API key not found'?
column2: Whether the key has been correctly added to the 'Secrets' section of the Space settings.

column1: How can you change the output of the AI to include relevant emojis?
column2: By adding instructions in the system prompt for the AI to include emojis in its response.

column1: Process: Implementing Security in Colab
column2: 1. Open 'Secrets' tab. 2. Add New Secret. 3. Name it. 4. Paste value. 5. Enable Notebook Access. 6. Access via Python code.

column1: What Python library acts as the official client for interacting with Groq models?
column2: groq

column1: Which Gradio component is used to display the final LinkedIn post to the user?
column2: Markdown or Textbox (set to output).

column1: What is the main advantage of using Hugging Face Spaces for hosting AI apps?
column2: It provides free (or low-cost) hosting specifically optimized for ML models and interfaces.

column1: Why should you avoid including the API key directly in a string like `api_key = "YOUR_KEY_HERE"` when sharing code?
column2: It leaks the credential to anyone who views the code.

column1: In Python, what character is used at the start of a line to run a terminal/shell command in a Jupyter notebook cell?
column2: Exclamation mark ($!$)

column1: What logic does the Gradio 'Launch' function execute?
column2: It starts the web server and provides the local or public URL to access the interface.

column1: How can you specify that an AI model should produce 'human-like' and 'impactful' posts?
column2: By including those specific descriptive keywords in the system instruction/prompt.

column1: True or False: Using GPT to generate code guarantees the code will work perfectly without any errors.
column2: False

column1: What is the benefit of the 'Clear' button in many Gradio app interfaces?
column2: It resets all input fields and outputs to their default, empty states.

column1: Which file in Hugging Face handles the 'metadata' such as the Space's title and license?
column2: README.md

column1: What happens if you use a 'paid' API (like OpenAI) without a valid credit card or balance?
column2: The API will return an error and the application will fail to generate content.

column1: What does the instructor suggest as a way to manage multiple Colab notebooks efficiently?
column2: Naming them clearly so they are searchable in the Google Drive 'Colab Notebooks' folder.
